topics.title: Beautification wave
topics.sub: beauty first, perf later · anchor: BG3 · realism climbs everywhere

deps.why: CHOSEN, mixed-grain: only the TSL code lifts (trees/grass/wind/clouds/sky/GI from fable5-world-demo, MIT) hard-require the new renderer — props/catalogs are renderer-agnostic — but building every visual packet once, on the final renderer, beats building twice

topics.title: World performance pass
topics.sub: instancing/LOD/entry-FPS after beautification (consciously deferred)

deps.why: Nothing to optimize before the props, trees and lighting exist — this pass instances/LODs what the wave adds

topics.title: Building generator
topics.sub: separate project; contract-first, emits data not meshes

topics.title: Fight in place — slice 1
topics.sub: kill the teleport — combat renders in the streamed world

deps.why: CHOSEN order, not a technical block: Remy ruled the world must be worth fighting in before combat moves into it (slice 1 would run on ugly ground)

topics.title: Ambush sites + dressing
topics.sub: ambush-worthy site selection along routes + seeded justified props

deps.why: Set dressing IS props: ambush dressing places entries through the prop schema + placement engine (with referee data) — no prop system, no dressing

topics.title: Mesh LOS + cover
topics.sub: multi-point raycasts → 5e cover tiers; LOCKED as single LOS oracle

deps.why: Could technically be built today against the battle map — sequenced after slice 1 so the oracle is tested against the real streamed scene it's designed for

topics.title: Battle-map retirement
topics.sub: after in-place passes the presentation-parity matrix

deps.why: The arena can only retire once in-place combat passes the presentation-parity matrix — it stays as the fallback until then
Placeless fights (travel ambushes, story encounters) need their streamed-real-spot replacement before the conjured arena dies

topics.title: Combat world reactions
topics.sub: the world answers for every fight

deps.why: Bystander disposition, death cascades and cleanup all react to fights happening IN the streamed world — they presume in-place combat exists

topics.title: AI arbiter (embodied DM)
topics.sub: full DM authority by thinking AS the NPC

deps.why: Thematic sequencing only: the arbiter's first named powers are combat story-hooks, but NPC embodiment itself is bigger than combat and could start independently

topics.title: Crossfire protection + arbiter override
topics.sub: civilians can't die by accident; only the arbiter may author a death as a story hook

deps.why: The protection rule is an exception INSIDE bystander disposition — who counts as a protected civilian is exactly what that feature decides, so it must exist first

The override half IS an arbiter power: authoring a civilian death requires the embodied-DM machinery (this used to hide as a feature in BOTH parents — split out 2026-07-02 because the map showed them as parallel when they are coupled)

features.title: Props wired into GroundWorld + combat extraction
Placement tuning: stall-in-building overlap + wilderness density explosion (130k/window)
Full-catalog bridge flip (placement draws the 105-def catalog, density governed)
PropPlacementContext enrichment: tavern / wealthy / gate / ruin / riverbank / defile contexts fire
Scatter de-gridding: grass tufts drop dotted-row artifact (decorrelated jitter + patchy noise density)

topics.title: Plan-map (this)

topics.title: WebGPURenderer migration
topics.sub: three.js WebGPU + TSL; unlocks MIT code lifts from fable5-world-demo; VALIDATED on the RTX 2070S (37fps headless, 15.5M tris)

features.title: WebGPU probe: streamed ground scene on WebGPURenderer
Material parity pass (terrain, water, buildings)
features.note: Root cause: R3F-mounted scene lights don't drive the WebGPU LightsNode (three #30044 / r3f #2853) -> zero irradiance -> black terrain. Fixed by baking hemisphere+sun Lambert into unlit MeshBasicNodeMaterial colorNodes (no scene lights). Verified on WebGL2-fallback node path; real-GPU eyeball still pending. Sky = fog+CSS backdrop (fine); postprocessing = backlog.

features.title: Probe full-stack parity
features.note: WHY (dep on the material parity pass): parity was proven only on flat terrain + building boxes while the ground world now ships trees, grass, the full prop set, styled roofs, walls/roads/decks. The probe (?phase=webgpuprobe) now renders the SAME full stack the live scene does at the same seed/pose, reusing the game's data (chunk bundles + GroundWorld.props) with the baked-TSL Lambert path. FAIL-FAST aligned with the battle map: pre-checks navigator.gpu + requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter OR a non-WebGPU backend -> NO scene, an error pane ('WebGPU unavailable: <reason>') with one explicit 'View this scene in WebGL instead' button (routes to ?phase=world3d&ground=1 at the same pose; user-driven, never auto-fallback). Green 'WebGPU' + live FPS badge only on genuine success. Documented MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen): real-time shadows on the node path (no LightsNode consumes a shadow map when lighting is baked into colorNode). Key node-path gotcha found + fixed: on InstancedMesh the InstanceNode multiplies instanceColor into the color pipeline UNCONDITIONALLY, so a dark per-instance scatter tint crushed lit trees/bushes to black — proven in isolation on a real WebGPU backend ([0,19,0] with dark instanceColor vs [109,158,88] without) and fixed by leaving instanceColor null (trees keep geometry leaf-green; bushes use a solid green). Headless capture correctly shows the error pane (no WebGPU adapter in headless Chromium); rendered WebGPU full-stack needs the real-GPU eyeball (RTX 2070S).

features.title: Battle-map + modal scenes migration
features.note: Battle-map WebGPU render path BUILT behind ?gpu=1 (WebGL default unchanged): BattleMap3D delegates to BattleMap3DGpuScene, same shared combat hooks/data, baked-TSL Lambert on terrain/water/grid/character tokens. FAIL-FAST: probes navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter -> NO scene, an error panel with reason + one explicit 'Use WebGL instead' button (remounts WebGL, strips gpu=1; user-driven, never auto-fallback); gl factory throws on a non-WebGPU backend. WebGPU badge only in the genuine success case. 7/7 tests incl. fail-fast suite; error panel + button remount live-verified headlessly; rendered WebGPU battlefield needs the real-GPU eyeball via ?gpu=1 (RTX 2070S). Documented parity GAPS (WebGL-only, no faking): real-time shadows, postprocessing bloom/vignette, procedural GLSL terrain texturing, grid movement/path/AoE overlay + targeting decals, VFX, grass/tree instancing, drei nameplates, animated CharacterActor rig. Modal (non-battle) scenes still parked.

topics.sub: GOAL: creator → town → living world; companions findable, travel works, shops have logical stock + prices; every L1-3 mechanic usable

features.title: Opening dialogue choice buttons dead (free text works)
Shop entry + trade UI at wf towns (NO path exists)
Roster townsfolk talkable (only opening NPC reachable)
Post-combat NPC state stale (beaten guard chats normally)
Companion encounters reachable in early game
Oracle = DM voice: story-so-far recap from runtime adventure log (currently errors silently)
Rumor stream repeats same 2 lines every minute
Recruit consent depth: strangers at disposition 50 auto-join (too easy)
Oracle must answer the player's actual question (generic recap suspected)
TTS error text leaks into game log
Terrain renders water-blue around towns (reproducible in ground demo)
Ollama modal promises fallback dialogue (contradicts no-fallback)
